title: Welfare and housing associate professionals n.e.c.
description: Counsel and advise individuals with alcohol, tobacco, drug, or other problems, such as gambling and eating disorders. May counsel individuals, families, or groups or engage in prevention programs. Advise and assist students and provide educational and vocational guidance services. Diagnose and treat mental and emotional disorders, whether cognitive, affective, or behavioral, within the context of marriage and family systems. Apply psychotherapeutic and family systems theories and techniques in the delivery of services to individuals, couples, and families for the purpose of treating such diagnosed nervous and mental disorders. (Based on 24 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Counsel individuals, groups, families, or communities regarding issues including mental health, substance abuse, and social adjustment.
Develop and implement individualized treatment plans for clients addressing personal issues and dependencies.
Assist clients in adhering to treatment plans by setting up appointments and providing necessary support or resources.
Interview clients, review records, and confer with professionals to assess individuals' conditions and determine required services.
Educate clients or community members about mental health issues, available resources, and how to manage personal challenges.
Collaborate with other professionals to coordinate counseling services and develop treatment plans for clients.
Monitor and record clients' progress to ensure that treatment goals and objectives are met effectively.
Provide public education and consultation regarding counseling services and mental health issues to other professionals or groups.
Assess clients' cognitive abilities and emotional needs to determine appropriate interventions and strategies.
Encourage clients to discuss their feelings and experiences, helping them gain insight into themselves and their relationships.
Conduct crisis interventions to assist clients in urgent situations and promote their safety.
Supervise and train new staff or volunteers, providing guidance on counseling methods and protocols.
Maintain case files including clients' personal information, treatment history, and progress documentation to ensure compliance with regulations.
Develop rehabilitation plans that align with clients' aptitudes, education levels, and career goals, coordinating with various agencies.
Facilitate collaboration with clients' families when implementing rehabilitation plans and understanding treatment progress.
Collect information about clients through interviews, observation, or tests.
Act as client advocates to coordinate required services or resolve emergency problems in crisis situations.
Develop and implement treatment plans based on clinical experience and knowledge.
Evaluate clients' physical or mental condition based on a review of client information.
Meet with families, probation officers, police, or other interested parties to exchange necessary information during treatment.
Counsel family members to assist them in understanding and supporting clients or patients.
Lead group counseling sessions that provide support in areas such as grief, stress, or chemical dependency.
Evaluate the effectiveness of counseling programs on clients' progress towards defined objectives and modify treatment as needed.
Monitor clients' use of medications and ensure compliance with treatment plans.
Gather information about community mental health needs or resources that can be integrated into therapy, and refer clients to community resources for services.
Plan or conduct programs to prevent substance abuse or improve community health and counseling services.
Counsel clients in individual or group sessions to help them deal with substance abuse, mental or physical illness, and adjust to life situations.
Collaborate with other professionals to evaluate clients' needs and coordinate treatment, ensuring service efficacy.
Monitor, evaluate, and record clients' progress with respect to treatment goals using measurable objectives.
Supervise and direct other workers who provide services to clients or patients and ensure quality of care.
Provide information or refer individuals to public or private agencies for assistance with mental health, housing, or financial needs.
Prepare and maintain case histories and reports to track client progress and service effectiveness.
Counsel individuals or groups to help them understand and overcome personal, social, or behavioral issues affecting their situation.
Conduct follow-up interviews with clients to determine if their needs have been met and adjust services accordingly.
Teach classes and present self-help or information sessions on subjects related to education and career planning.
Monitor and manage inventory, including ordering materials, tracking requisitions, and verifying shipment accuracy.
Maintain knowledge of organizational and governmental rules affecting purchases and provide information to staff and vendors.
Locate and interview suppliers to gather product information and compare prices and specifications to ensure the best purchase decisions.
Prepare orders, review bills, and respond to inquiries about order statuses while managing billing and account transactions.
Compile, copy, sort, and file records of office activities, business transactions, and maintain filing and inventory systems.
Review files, records, and documents to gather information for responses to requests, and manage communication through mail and phone.
Train and supervise staff, including preparing meeting agendas, recording minutes, and processing office documents.
Troubleshoot problems with office equipment and train staff on using computer applications.
